title: FIGS Q1 2022 Earnings Call - Supply Chain Analysis
text: FIGS Q1 2022 earnings call focused on unprecedented supply chain disruption beginning early March 2022. Company experienced volatile ocean transit times ranging 30 to 120+ days with unexpectedly rerouted vessels. Unpredictability forced product launches out of Q1 and created stockouts on key styles like Zamora and Yola pants. Management stated: Shipping times ranged from 30 days to 120+ days with difficulty seeing unpredictability ending soon. Gross margins declined 40 basis points YoY to 71.2% from increased airfreight and elevated freight rates. Full-year 2022 guidance revised to expect 67-68% gross margins down from 70%+ prior, a 300-400 basis point reduction. Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance lowered to 16-18% from 20%+ target, reflecting supply chain headwinds and increased strategic investments. Management acknowledged consumers facing macroeconomic pressure and inventory constraints being primary factor affecting full-year outlook.
